name: Amish Friendship Yeast Starter
recipeInstructions:
DAY ONE: In a glass or ceramic bowl, mix together thoroughly. Leave on the kitchen counter uncovered; don't refrigerate it.
DAYS 2, 3, and 4: Stir well with wooden spoon.
DAY FIVE: Stir and add 1 cup milk, 1 cup flour, and 1 cup sugar (called "feeding the starter").
DAYS 6, 7, and 8: Stir well with wooden spoon.
DAY NINE: Stir and add 1 cup milk, 1 cup flour, and 1 cup sugar; stir well.
DAYS 10 and 11: Stir well with wooden spoon.
DAY TWELVE: Ladle 1 cup starter into each of 4 containers and refrigerate. Use one for baking Friendship bread or cake recipes, keep one for later use, and give two to friends. Include all recipes when giving the starter.
If not baking on this day, add 1 teaspoon granulated sugar and refrigerate. Every 10 days, remove from refrigerator, transfer to bowl, feed with 1 cup milk, 1 cup flour, and 1 cup sugar, leave uncovered for 2 days, then bake or divide among friends and save some. Discard or freeze (thaw before use).
Note: If starter turns pink, discard and start over. Use same flour and milk when adding ingredients.
WARNING: Do not kill the heat; do not smash; avoid burn, splutter, or destroy.
